---
title: "MRP"
author: "Jacob_Bowie"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:  
   html_document:
    fig_width: 10
    fig_height: 6
    dpi: 300
    fig_format: "png"
    text_size: 12
---

# Maximal returns protocol (MRP) analysis

```{r Load and format data, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE)
set.seed(0308)
# Function to install and load packages efficiently
install_and_load_packages <- function(packages) {
  # Check which packages are not installed
  new_packages <- packages[!packages %in% installed.packages()[,"Package"]]
  
  # Install missing packages if any
  if(length(new_packages)) {
    install.packages(new_packages)
  }
  # Load all packages, with suppressed messages
  invisible(sapply(packages, function(pkg) {
    suppressMessages(library(pkg, character.only = TRUE))
  }))
  # Report which packages were newly installed
  if(length(new_packages)) {
    message("Newly installed packages: ", paste(new_packages, collapse = ", "))
  }}
# Updated package list with recommended additions and removing duplicates
required_packages <- c(
  # Data handling
  "tidyverse", "readxl", "writexl", "openxlsx", "dplyr", "tidyr", "stringr", "purrr",
  # Statistical analysis
  "car", "broom", "lme4", "emmeans", "effectsize", 
  # Visualization
  "ggplot2", "ggpubr", "ggsignif", "cowplot", "gridExtra", "ggrepel", 
  "corrplot", "ggtext", "ggsci", "viridis",
  # Reporting
  "knitr", "kableExtra", "flextable"
)
# Remove any duplicates
required_packages <- unique(required_packages)
# Install and load packages
install_and_load_packages(required_packages)
# Get the current date and format it as mmddyyyy
date <- format(Sys.Date(), "%m%d%Y")
setwd("G:/My Drive/UCONN/Publications or Projects/MRP/MRP")
# Create the directory name with todays date and check if it exists
output_directory <- paste( date, sep = "_","output")
if (!dir.exists(output_directory)) {
  dir.create(output_directory)}

# Define color values
dark_blue_color <- "#00205B"   # Dark blue color
light_blue_color <- "#6699CC"  # Light blue color
third_color <- "#A0C9E0"       # Third color
lbs_to_kg <- 0.45359237

# Read in the Excel file and perform all transformations in a single pipeline
mydata <- read_excel("MRP.xlsx", sheet = "Strength", col_names = TRUE) %>%
  select(1:3, 7:9) %>%
  rename(Subject = 1, Measure = 2, Exercise = 3, PRE = 4, MID = 5, POST = 6) %>%
  filter(!(Subject %in% c("SUB 5", "SUB 13"))) %>%  # Remove subjects 5 and 13 due to missing data
  mutate(
    PRE = as.numeric(PRE),
    MID = as.numeric(MID),
    POST = as.numeric(POST)  )

# Define the file name with the current date
file_name <- paste0(output_directory, "/", date, "Strength_raw_data.xlsx")
write_xlsx(mydata, file_name)

# Define the replacements
replacements <- c(
  "Overhead Press" = "OHP",
  "Lat Pull down" = "Lat Pull",
  "Leg Press" = "Leg Press",
  "Bent Over Row" = "Bent Row",
  "Seated Row" = "Seated Row",
  "Lat Pulldown" = "Lat Pull")
# Apply the replacements to the Exercise column
mydata$Exercise <- dplyr::recode(mydata$Exercise, !!!replacements)
upper_exercises <- c("OHP", "Lat Pull", "Bent Row", "Seated Row")
lower_exercises <- c("Leg Press", "Deadlift")

# Define the subjects that are trained and untrained
trained_subjects <- c(8, 17, 18, 19)
untrained_subjects <- c(2, 3, 10, 11, 14)
trained_subjects <- paste0("SUB ", trained_subjects)
untrained_subjects <- paste0("SUB ", untrained_subjects)

# Add a new column "TrainingStatus" based on the subjects
mydata <- mydata %>%
  mutate(TrainingStatus = ifelse(Subject %in% trained_subjects, "trained", "untrained"))

mydata_combined <- mydata %>%
  mutate(Measure_Exercise = paste(Measure, Exercise, sep = "_")) %>%
  filter(Measure_Exercise != "1RM_Bent Row" & Measure_Exercise != "10RM_Bent Row")

# Fix the duplicated entries in mydata_combined
mydata_combined_fixed <- mydata_combined %>%
  filter(!(Subject == "SUB 3" & 
           Measure == "10RM" & 
           Exercise == "Seated Row" & 
           PRE == 90.7 & 
           MID == 93.0 & 
           POST == 95.3))
```

## Assessing Normality and homogeneity of variance

```{r Assessing Normality and homogeneity of variance, echo=FALSE}
# Pivot the data into a longer format
mydata_long <- pivot_longer(mydata_combined, cols = c(PRE, MID, POST),
                            names_to = "Time", values_to = "Value")

# Group  data by 'Measure_Exercise' and perform the Shapiro-Wilk test for each group
normality_tests <- mydata_long %>%
  group_by(Measure_Exercise) %>%
  summarize(p_value = shapiro.test(Value)$p.value, .groups = 'drop')
# Print the results
kable(normality_tests, digits = 3, caption = "Shapiro-Wilk test for normality group_by(Measure_Exercise) reveals measures are normally distributed ")

# Create a histogram facetted by "Measure_Exercise" and colored by Time
ggplot(mydata_long, aes(x = Value, fill = Time)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 2, position = "dodge") +
  facet_wrap(~ Measure_Exercise, scales = "free") +  # Updated to allow for free scales if necessary
  labs(x = "Value", y = "Frequency") +
  ggtitle("Histogram Facetted by Measure_Exercise and Colored by Time") +
  theme_pubr()

```



```{r Assessing Normality and homogeneity of variance2, echo=FALSE}
normality_tests <- mydata_long %>%
  group_by(Measure_Exercise, TrainingStatus) %>%
  summarize(p_value = shapiro.test(Value)$p.value, .groups = 'drop')
# Print the results
filter(normality_tests, p_value < 0.05) %>%
  kable(digits = 3, caption = "Shapiro-Wilk test for normality group_by(Measure_Exercise, TrainingStatus) ")

# Create a histogram facetted by "Measure_Exercise" and colored by Time
mydata_filtered <- mydata_long %>%
  filter(Measure_Exercise %in% c( "10RM_Lat Pull", "1RM_Seated Row"	)) %>%
  filter(TrainingStatus == "untrained")
ggplot(mydata_filtered, aes(x = Value, fill = Time)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 2, position = "dodge") +
  facet_wrap(~ Measure_Exercise, scales = "free") +  # Updated to allow for free scales if necessary
  ggtitle("Histogram of non-normal measures by training status") +
   theme_pubr()
```



```{r Assessing Normality and homogeneity of variance3, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Group data by 'Measure_Exercise' and 'Time' perform the Shapiro-Wilk test for each group
normality_tests <- mydata_long %>%
  group_by(Measure_Exercise, Time) %>%
  summarize(p_value = shapiro.test(Value)$p.value, .groups = 'drop')
# Filter for sig non-normality 
filtered_normality_tests <- filter(normality_tests, p_value < 0.05)
kable(filtered_normality_tests, digits = 3, caption = "Shapiro-Wilk test for non-normality group_by(Measure_Exercise, Time) reveals OHP and Seated Row are not normally distributed at specific timepoints")
```



```{r Assessing Normality and homogeneity of variance4, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
normality_tests <- mydata_long %>%
  group_by(Measure_Exercise, Time, TrainingStatus) %>%
  filter(n() >= 3) %>%  # Ensure each group has at least 3 observations
  summarize(
    p_value = shapiro.test(Value)$p.value,
    .groups = 'drop'  # Drop the grouping structure after summarizing
  )

# Filter for sig non-normality 
filtered_normality_tests <- filter(normality_tests, p_value < 0.05)
kable(filtered_normality_tests, digits = 3, caption = "Shapiro-Wilk test for non-normality group_by(Measure_Exercise, Time, TrainingStatus)")
```

Significantly not normal distribution at select timepoints. Slight right skewness in the data, but the data is not too far from normality. 
OHP has a very tight range, with some very low values the extra weak subjects skewing the data 

```{r Histograms, echo=FALSE}
# Filter for sig non-normality 
mydata_long %>%
  filter(Measure_Exercise %in% c("10RM_OHP", "1RM_Seated Row", "10RM_Seated Row"))%>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Value, fill = Time)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 5, position = "dodge") +  # Set position to "dodge" to unstack bars
  facet_wrap(~ Measure_Exercise, scales = "free") +
  labs(x = "Value", y = "Frequency") +
  ggtitle("Histogram Facetted by Measure_Exercise") +
  theme_pubr()

#Filter for sig non-normality by training status
mydata_long %>%
  filter(Measure_Exercise %in% c("10RM_Seated Row", "1RM_Seated Row", "10RM_Leg Press", "10RM_Lat Pull"))%>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Value, fill = TrainingStatus, color = Time)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 5, position = "dodge") +  # Set position to "dodge" to unstack bars
  facet_wrap(~ Measure_Exercise, scales = "free") +
  labs(x = "Value", y = "Frequency") +
  ggtitle("Histogram Facetted by Measure_Exercise and TrainingStatus") +
  theme_pubr()

```

1RM_Seated Row untrained had once participant with a very low value, skewing the data. (SUB 11 POST 36.3 whereas average is about 90 for the other participants)
10RM_Seated Row Trained had one participant outlier with 100 vs avg of 70 
10RM_Lat Pull untrained had two participants with 31.8, 45.4, vs 68 in other three participants
10RM_Leg Press untrained had the one weak participant
10RM_Leg Press trained had one extra strong participant
Small sample size, so outliers have a large impact on the data

## Perform Levene's Test for homogeneity of variances

```{r Levenes Test Measure_exercise, echo=TRUE}
leveneTest(Value ~ Time * Measure_Exercise, data = mydata_long)
leveneTest(Value ~ Time * Measure_Exercise * TrainingStatus, data = mydata_long)
```

Variance is homogenous

## Perform Levene's Test for each Measure

```{r Levenes Test, echo=TRUE, warning=FALSE}
levene_results <- mydata_long %>%
  group_by(Measure_Exercise ) %>%
  do(tidy(leveneTest(Value ~ Time, data = .)))
filtered_levene_results <- filter(levene_results, p.value < 0.05)
kable(filtered_levene_results, digits = 3)
#no significant differences in variance
levene_results <- mydata_long %>%
  group_by(Measure_Exercise, TrainingStatus ) %>%
  do(tidy(leveneTest(Value ~ Time, data = .)))
filtered_levene_results <- filter(levene_results, p.value < 0.05)
kable(filtered_levene_results, digits = 3)
```

Variance is homogenous for each measure
1RM_OHP trained has a significant difference in variance (p=0.049), but the sample size is small and the effect is barely <0.05

#### Step 1: ANOVA 

```{r ANOVA, echo=FALSE}
# Combine Measure and Exercise columns into a unified identifier
mydata_mutate <- mydata %>%
  filter(Exercise != "Bent Row") %>%
  unite("Measure", Measure, Exercise, sep = "_") 

# Reshape to long format for analysis
mydata_reshaped <- mydata_mutate %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c("PRE", "MID", "POST"), names_to = "Timepoint", values_to = "Weight") %>%
  mutate(Timepoint = factor(Timepoint, levels = c("PRE", "MID", "POST")))

# Create a function to run and extract ANOVA results
run_anova <- function(data, formula, filter_var = NULL, filter_value = NULL) {
  # Apply filter if provided
  if (!is.null(filter_var) && !is.null(filter_value)) {
    data <- data %>% filter(!!sym(filter_var) == filter_value)
  }
  # Get unique measures
  measures <- unique(data$Measure)
  
  # Initialize results data frame
  results <- data.frame()
  
  # Loop through measures
  for (measure in measures) {
    # Subset data for current measure
    measure_data <- data %>% filter(Measure == measure)
    
    # Fit model and extract ANOVA table
    model <- lm(formula, data = measure_data)
    anova_result <- anova(model)
    
    # Process results with broom
    tidy_result <- tidy(anova_result) %>%
      mutate(
        Measure = measure,
        filter_condition = if(is.null(filter_value)) "all" else filter_value
      )
    # Add to results
    results <- bind_rows(results, tidy_result)
  }
  return(results)
}
# Run different models
# 1. Full model with interaction
full_model_results <- run_anova(
  mydata_reshaped, 
  Weight ~ Timepoint * TrainingStatus + Subject
)
# 2. Model without interaction (all subjects)
main_effects_results <- run_anova(
  mydata_reshaped, 
  Weight ~ Timepoint + Subject
)
# 3. Trained subjects only
trained_results <- run_anova(
  mydata_reshaped, 
  Weight ~ Timepoint + Subject,
  "TrainingStatus", "trained"
)
# 4. Untrained subjects only
untrained_results <- run_anova(
  mydata_reshaped, 
  Weight ~ Timepoint + Subject,
  "TrainingStatus", "untrained"
)
# Combine all results with labels for clearer reporting
all_anova_results <- bind_rows(
  full_model_results %>% mutate(model_type = "Full model with interaction"),
  main_effects_results %>% mutate(model_type = "Main effects only"),
  trained_results %>% mutate(model_type = "Trained subjects only"),
  untrained_results %>% mutate(model_type = "Untrained subjects only")
)
# Add post-hoc tests for significant effects
# Create a function for post-hoc analysis
run_posthoc <- function(data, measure_name) {
  measure_data <- data %>% filter(Measure == measure_name)
  
  # Create model for emmeans
  model <- lm(Weight ~ Timepoint * TrainingStatus + Subject, data = measure_data)
  
  # Run pairwise comparisons
  posthoc <- emmeans(model, pairwise ~ Timepoint, adjust = "tukey")
  
  # Extract and format results
  posthoc_results <- tidy(posthoc$contrasts) %>%
    mutate(Measure = measure_name)
  
  return(posthoc_results)
}
# Identify measures with significant time effects
sig_measures <- full_model_results %>%
  filter(term == "Timepoint", p.value < 0.05) %>%
  pull(Measure)

# Run post-hoc tests for significant measures
posthoc_results <- map_df(sig_measures, ~run_posthoc(mydata_reshaped, .))

# Create formatted tables with kableExtra
full_model_table <- full_model_results %>%
  filter(term %in% c("Timepoint", "TrainingStatus", "Timepoint:TrainingStatus")) %>%
  select(Measure, term, statistic, p.value) %>%
  mutate(significance = case_when(
    p.value < 0.001 ~ "***",
    p.value < 0.01 ~ "**", 
    p.value < 0.05 ~ "*",
    TRUE ~ "ns"
  )) %>%
  arrange(Measure, term)

# Display results with kableExtra for better formatting
full_model_table %>%
  kbl(digits = 3, caption = "ANOVA results for each measure (Weight ~ Timepoint * TrainingStatus + Subject)") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
  pack_rows(index = table(full_model_table$Measure))

# Display post-hoc results for significant time effects
posthoc_results %>%
  filter(adj.p.value < 0.05) %>%
  mutate(
    comparison = str_replace_all(contrast, " - ", " vs "),
    p.value = round(adj.p.value, 3),
    significance = case_when(
      adj.p.value < 0.001 ~ "***",
      adj.p.value < 0.01 ~ "**", 
      adj.p.value < 0.05 ~ "*",
      TRUE ~ "ns"
    )
  ) %>%
  select(Measure, comparison, estimate, p.value, significance) %>%
  kbl(caption = "Significant pairwise comparisons between timepoints") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
  pack_rows(index = table(posthoc_results$Measure[posthoc_results$adj.p.value < 0.05]))

# Save comprehensive results to file if needed
write_xlsx(list(
  "Full Model" = full_model_results,
  "Main Effects" = main_effects_results,
  "Trained Only" = trained_results,
  "Untrained Only" = untrained_results,
  "Post-Hoc Tests" = posthoc_results
), paste0(output_directory, "/", date, "_comprehensive_anova_results.xlsx"))
```

The analysis reveals significant effects of time (PRE, MID, POST) on strength performance across all exercises (both 1RM and 10RM), indicating that the training intervention was effective in producing strength adaptations. All 12 measures showed statistically significant improvements over time (p < 0.05).
Training status was a significant factor for most exercises, with 9 out of 12 measures showing significant differences between trained and untrained individuals. The exceptions were 10RM_Seated Row, 1RM_Leg Press, and 10RM_Leg Press (though this last one was still significant at p=0.037).
Interestingly, there was minimal evidence for interaction effects between training status and time. Only 1RM_Lat Pull showed a significant interaction (p=0.023), suggesting that for this exercise, trained and untrained individuals responded differently to the intervention. For all other exercises, both groups showed similar patterns of improvement despite their different baseline levels.
The pairwise comparisons reveal that significant strength improvements occurred both from PRE to MID and from MID to POST timepoints, demonstrating progressive adaptation throughout the intervention period. Many exercises showed continued improvements in the later phase of training (MID to POST), indicating that strength adaptations were still occurring and hadn't plateaued.
The warning messages about nesting structure (Subject within TrainingStatus) suggest your statistical model appropriately accounts for the fact that subjects are nested within their training status group, but interpretation requires caution due to the involvement in interactions.
These findings support that:

The resistance training program effectively improved strength across multiple exercises
Training effects were similar for both trained and untrained individuals (parallel improvement trajectories)
Both 1RM and 10RM measures responded positively to training
Strength gains continued throughout the duration of the program

This suggests that the training program was well-designed to elicit progressive adaptations regardless of initial training status, with only lat pulldown showing evidence of differential responses between trained and untrained participants.

# Step 2: Calculate mean, standard deviation, and mean±SD for PRE, MID, POST and calculate p-values

```{r Summary Stats, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
# Calculate summary statistics for all subjects
mydata_summary <- mydata_combined %>%
  group_by(Measure_Exercise) %>%
  summarize(
    PRE_Mean = mean(PRE, na.rm = TRUE),
    PRE_SD = sd(PRE, na.rm = TRUE),
    PRE_n = sum(!is.na(PRE)),
    MID_Mean = mean(MID, na.rm = TRUE),
    MID_SD = sd(MID, na.rm = TRUE),
    MID_n = sum(!is.na(MID)),
    POST_Mean = mean(POST, na.rm = TRUE),
    POST_SD = sd(POST, na.rm = TRUE),
    POST_n = sum(!is.na(POST)),
    PRE_Mean_SD = paste0(round(PRE_Mean, 2), "\u00B1", round(PRE_SD, 2)),
    MID_Mean_SD = paste0(round(MID_Mean, 2), "\u00B1", round(MID_SD, 2)),
    POST_Mean_SD = paste0(round(POST_Mean, 2), "\u00B1", round(POST_SD, 2)),
    .groups = 'drop'
  )

# Calculate Hedge's g (bias-corrected effect size for small samples)
mydata_summary <- mydata_summary %>%
  mutate(
    # Hedge's g for PRE to MID
    hedges_g_pre_mid = ((MID_Mean - PRE_Mean) / 
                        sqrt((((PRE_n - 1) * PRE_SD^2) + ((MID_n - 1) * MID_SD^2)) / 
                             (PRE_n + MID_n - 2))) * 
                        (1 - (3 / (4 * (PRE_n + MID_n - 2) - 1))),
    
    # Hedge's g for PRE to POST
    hedges_g_pre_post = ((POST_Mean - PRE_Mean) / 
                         sqrt((((PRE_n - 1) * PRE_SD^2) + ((POST_n - 1) * POST_SD^2)) / 
                              (PRE_n + POST_n - 2))) * 
                         (1 - (3 / (4 * (PRE_n + POST_n - 2) - 1))),
    
    # Hedge's g for MID to POST
    hedges_g_mid_post = ((POST_Mean - MID_Mean) / 
                         sqrt((((MID_n - 1) * MID_SD^2) + ((POST_n - 1) * POST_SD^2)) / 
                              (MID_n + POST_n - 2))) * 
                         (1 - (3 / (4 * (MID_n + POST_n - 2) - 1)))
  ) %>%
  select(-PRE_Mean, -PRE_SD, -PRE_n, -MID_Mean, -MID_SD, -MID_n, -POST_Mean, -POST_SD, -POST_n)

# Create long-format data for statistical testing
mydata_long <- mydata_combined %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c("PRE", "MID", "POST"), names_to = "Timepoint", values_to = "Weight") %>%
  mutate(Timepoint = factor(Timepoint, levels = c("PRE", "MID", "POST")))

# Function to run paired t-tests for each timepoint pair
# Function to run paired t-tests with multiple grouping variables
run_paired_ttests_grouped <- function(data, group_vars) {
  # Create a combined grouping variable
  data$GroupID <- do.call(paste, c(data[group_vars], sep = "___"))
  
  # Get all unique group combinations
  unique_groups <- unique(data$GroupID)
  
  # Prepare results dataframe
  results <- data.frame(
    GroupID = character(),
    p_val_pre_mid = numeric(),
    p_val_pre_post = numeric(),
    p_val_mid_post = numeric(),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  # For each unique group combination
  for (g in unique_groups) {
    # Filter data for this group
    group_data <- subset(data, GroupID == g)
    
    # Match subjects across timepoints to ensure proper pairing
    pre_subj <- group_data$Subject[group_data$Timepoint == "PRE"]
    mid_subj <- group_data$Subject[group_data$Timepoint == "MID"]
    post_subj <- group_data$Subject[group_data$Timepoint == "POST"]
    
    # Find common subjects for each comparison
    pre_mid_subj <- intersect(pre_subj, mid_subj)
    pre_post_subj <- intersect(pre_subj, post_subj)
    mid_post_subj <- intersect(mid_subj, post_subj)
    
    # Get matched data
    pre_mid_pre <- sapply(pre_mid_subj, function(s) {
      group_data$Weight[group_data$Timepoint == "PRE" & group_data$Subject == s]
    })
    pre_mid_mid <- sapply(pre_mid_subj, function(s) {
      group_data$Weight[group_data$Timepoint == "MID" & group_data$Subject == s]
    })
    
    pre_post_pre <- sapply(pre_post_subj, function(s) {
      group_data$Weight[group_data$Timepoint == "PRE" & group_data$Subject == s]
    })
    pre_post_post <- sapply(pre_post_subj, function(s) {
      group_data$Weight[group_data$Timepoint == "POST" & group_data$Subject == s]
    })
    
    mid_post_mid <- sapply(mid_post_subj, function(s) {
      group_data$Weight[group_data$Timepoint == "MID" & group_data$Subject == s]
    })
    mid_post_post <- sapply(mid_post_subj, function(s) {
      group_data$Weight[group_data$Timepoint == "POST" & group_data$Subject == s]
    })
    
    # Conduct paired t-tests
    p_pre_mid <- if(length(pre_mid_pre) > 1) {
      tryCatch(
        t.test(pre_mid_pre, pre_mid_mid, paired = TRUE)$p.value,
        error = function(e) NA_real_
      )
    } else NA_real_
    
    p_pre_post <- if(length(pre_post_pre) > 1) {
      tryCatch(
        t.test(pre_post_pre, pre_post_post, paired = TRUE)$p.value,
        error = function(e) NA_real_
      )
    } else NA_real_
    
    p_mid_post <- if(length(mid_post_mid) > 1) {
      tryCatch(
        t.test(mid_post_mid, mid_post_post, paired = TRUE)$p.value,
        error = function(e) NA_real_
      )
    } else NA_real_
    
    # Extract the original group values
    group_values <- group_data[1, group_vars, drop = FALSE]
    
    # Add to results
    result_row <- data.frame(
      p_val_pre_mid = p_pre_mid,
      p_val_pre_post = p_pre_post,
      p_val_mid_post = p_mid_post
    )
    result_row <- cbind(group_values, result_row)
    
    results <- rbind(results, result_row)
  }
  
  return(results)
}
# Calculate p-values for all measures (without considering training status)
p_values <- run_paired_ttests_grouped(
  mydata_long, 
  "Measure_Exercise"  # Just pass a single variable as a character vector
)# Join summary stats and p-values
summary_p_values1 <- left_join(mydata_summary, p_values, by = "Measure_Exercise")

# Calculate summary statistics by training status
mydata_summary_TvsU <- mydata_combined %>%
  group_by(Measure_Exercise, TrainingStatus) %>%
  summarize(
    PRE_Mean = mean(PRE, na.rm = TRUE),
    PRE_SD = sd(PRE, na.rm = TRUE),
    PRE_n = sum(!is.na(PRE)),
    MID_Mean = mean(MID, na.rm = TRUE),
    MID_SD = sd(MID, na.rm = TRUE),
    MID_n = sum(!is.na(MID)),
    POST_Mean = mean(POST, na.rm = TRUE),
    POST_SD = sd(POST, na.rm = TRUE),
    POST_n = sum(!is.na(POST)),
    PRE_Mean_SD = paste0(round(PRE_Mean, 2), "\u00B1", round(PRE_SD, 2)),
    MID_Mean_SD = paste0(round(MID_Mean, 2), "\u00B1", round(MID_SD, 2)),
    POST_Mean_SD = paste0(round(POST_Mean, 2), "\u00B1", round(POST_SD, 2)),
    .groups = 'drop'
  )

# Calculate Hedge's g by training status
mydata_summary_TvsU <- mydata_summary_TvsU %>%
  mutate(
    # Hedge's g for PRE to MID
    hedges_g_pre_mid = ((MID_Mean - PRE_Mean) / 
                        sqrt((((PRE_n - 1) * PRE_SD^2) + ((MID_n - 1) * MID_SD^2)) / 
                             (PRE_n + MID_n - 2))) * 
                        (1 - (3 / (4 * (PRE_n + MID_n - 2) - 1))),
    
    # Hedge's g for PRE to POST
    hedges_g_pre_post = ((POST_Mean - PRE_Mean) / 
                         sqrt((((PRE_n - 1) * PRE_SD^2) + ((POST_n - 1) * POST_SD^2)) / 
                              (PRE_n + POST_n - 2))) * 
                         (1 - (3 / (4 * (PRE_n + POST_n - 2) - 1))),
    
    # Hedge's g for MID to POST
    hedges_g_mid_post = ((POST_Mean - MID_Mean) / 
                         sqrt((((MID_n - 1) * MID_SD^2) + ((POST_n - 1) * POST_SD^2)) / 
                              (MID_n + POST_n - 2))) * 
                         (1 - (3 / (4 * (MID_n + POST_n - 2) - 1)))
  ) %>%
  select(-PRE_Mean, -PRE_SD, -PRE_n, -MID_Mean, -MID_SD, -MID_n, -POST_Mean, -POST_SD, -POST_n)

# Calculate p-values by training status
# Calculate p-values by training status (considering both Measure_Exercise and TrainingStatus)
p_values_by_status <- run_paired_ttests_grouped(
  mydata_long %>% filter(Measure_Exercise != "10RM_Bench Press" | TrainingStatus != "trained"),
  c("Measure_Exercise", "TrainingStatus")
)
# Join summary stats and p-values for training status comparison
summary_p_values2 <- left_join(
  mydata_summary_TvsU, 
  p_values_by_status, 
  by = c("Measure_Exercise", "TrainingStatus")
)

# Format p-values for display
format_pvalue <- function(p) {
  if(is.na(p)) return("N/A")
  if(p < 0.001) return("<0.001***")
  if(p < 0.01) return(paste0(round(p, 3), "**"))
  if(p < 0.05) return(paste0(round(p, 3), "*"))
  return(as.character(round(p, 3)))
}

# Create tables with formatted p-values
summary_p_values1_table <- summary_p_values1 %>%
  mutate(
    p_val_pre_mid = map_chr(p_val_pre_mid, format_pvalue),
    p_val_pre_post = map_chr(p_val_pre_post, format_pvalue),
    p_val_mid_post = map_chr(p_val_mid_post, format_pvalue)
  )

summary_p_values2_table <- summary_p_values2 %>%
  mutate(
    p_val_pre_mid = map_chr(p_val_pre_mid, format_pvalue),
    p_val_pre_post = map_chr(p_val_pre_post, format_pvalue),
    p_val_mid_post = map_chr(p_val_mid_post, format_pvalue)
  )

# Display tables with kableExtra for better formatting
summary_p_values1_table %>%
  kbl(caption = "Summary of strength measures with p-values") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
  column_spec(1, bold = TRUE)

summary_p_values2_table %>%
  kbl(caption = "Trained Vs. Untrained Summary of strength measures with p-values") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
  column_spec(1, bold = TRUE) %>%
  column_spec(2, background = ifelse(summary_p_values2_table$TrainingStatus == "trained", "#e6f7ff", "#fff2e6"))

# Save Excel files
file_name_summary <- paste0(output_directory, "/", date, "strength_summary_pval.xlsx")
write_xlsx(summary_p_values1, file_name_summary)

file_name_summary_TvsU <- paste0(output_directory, "/", date, "strength_summary_TvsU_pval.xlsx")
write_xlsx(summary_p_values2, file_name_summary_TvsU)

# Compare trained vs untrained at each timepoint
comparison_results <- mydata_long %>%
  group_by(Measure_Exercise, Timepoint) %>%
  summarize(
    t_test = list(tryCatch(
      t.test(Weight ~ TrainingStatus, data = cur_data()),
      error = function(e) list(p.value = NA_real_)
    )),
    p_value = t_test[[1]]$p.value,
    .groups = 'drop'
  ) %>%
  select(Measure_Exercise, Timepoint, p_value)

# View significant differences
TvsU <- comparison_results %>% 
  filter(p_value < 0.1) %>%
  mutate(significance = case_when(
    p_value < 0.001 ~ "***",
    p_value < 0.01 ~ "**",
    p_value < 0.05 ~ "*",
    TRUE ~ "\u2020"  # Unicode dagger symbol for p < 0.1
  ))
if(nrow(TvsU) > 0) {
  TvsU %>%
    kbl(
      digits = 3, 
      caption = "Differences between trained and untrained (p < 0.1)",
      col.names = c("Measure", "Timepoint", "p-value", "Significance")
    ) %>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE)
}
# Plot example for benchpress10rm
benchpress_data <- mydata_long %>%
  filter(Measure_Exercise == "10RM_Bench Press", TrainingStatus == "trained")

if(nrow(benchpress_data) > 0) {
  plot_data <- benchpress_data %>%
    group_by(Timepoint) %>%
    summarize(
      Mean = mean(Weight, na.rm = TRUE),
      SD = sd(Weight, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = 'drop'
    )
  # Define the order of the x-axis
  timepoint_order <- c("PRE", "MID", "POST")
  
  # Create basic ggplot2 plot to avoid potential ggpubr issues
  benchpress10rm_plot <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = factor(Timepoint, levels = timepoint_order), y = Mean)) +
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = Mean - SD, ymax = Mean + SD), width = 0.2, color = "black") +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "lightblue", color = "black", width = 0.7) +
    labs(title = "BenchPress 10RM in Trained",
         x = "Timepoint",
         y = "Weight (kg)") +
    theme_pubr() +
    theme(
      panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      axis.title = element_text(face = "bold")
    )
    print(benchpress10rm_plot)
}
```

## Interpretation of Strength Training Results

The analysis reveals several important findings about the effects of our resistance training program on strength measures across different exercises, comparing trained versus untrained individuals.

### Overall Strength Improvements

All strength measures showed significant improvements over the training period. For the combined group analysis:

- Every exercise showed significant increases from PRE to POST (p < 0.01 for all measures)
- Most exercises showed significant improvements between all timepoints (PRE-MID, MID-POST, and PRE-POST)
- The effect sizes (Hedge's g) for PRE to POST changes were substantial, ranging from moderate (0.49 for 1RM Bench Press) to large (1.54 for 10RM Deadlift)

### Trained vs. Untrained Comparisons

When comparing trained vs. untrained participants:

1. Similar patterns of improvement were observed in both groups, with most exercises showing significant increases across timepoints
2. Effect sizes were generally larger in trained individuals, particularly for:
   - 10RM Deadlift (trained g=2.37 vs. untrained g=1.13)
   - 10RM Leg Press (trained g=2.09 vs. untrained g=0.92)
   - 10RM OHP (trained g=2.07 vs. untrained g=0.62)

3. Differential timing of adaptations:
   - Trained individuals often showed larger gains in the early phase (PRE-MID)
   - Some exercises (e.g., 1RM Bench Press, 1RM OHP) showed significant PRE-POST improvements in untrained individuals while trained individuals had p-values approaching but not reaching significance

4. Between-group differences were minimal, with only three exercises showing marginally significant differences (p < 0.1) between groups:
   - 10RM Lat Pull (MID and POST)
   - 1RM Lat Pull (POST)

### Exercise-Specific Findings

1. Deadlift showed particularly large improvements in both groups, especially for 10RM measures
2. Bench Press showed consistent improvements but with smaller effect sizes compared to other exercises
3. Overhead Press (OHP) showed notably different responses between trained and untrained groups
4. Lat Pull was one of the few exercises showing marginally significant differences between trained and untrained individuals

### Physiological Implications

The continued adaptations throughout the training period suggest that both trained and untrained individuals were responsive to the program. Higher relative gains in trained individuals for some exercises challenges the common assumption that untrained individuals always show greater relative improvements. The similar patterns of adaptation suggest that the underlying physiological mechanisms for strength gain may be consistent regardless of training status.

### Practical Applications

The program was effective for both trained and untrained individuals, making it suitable for mixed-experience groups. The continued improvements from MID to POST indicate that the program duration was appropriate for eliciting ongoing adaptations. The differences in effect sizes between exercises suggest that some movements (especially deadlifts) may be particularly responsive to training.



<!-- #### Bench press couldn't perform T-test because data were essentially constant  -->

# Step 3: Calculate percent change








```{r Percent Change, echo=FALSE}
# Define a function to calculate percent change
calculate_percent_change <- function(pre, mid, post) {
  data.frame(
    PRE_to_MID = ((mid - pre) / pre) * 100,
    MID_to_POST = ((post - mid) / mid) * 100,
    PRE_to_POST = ((post - pre) / pre) * 100  )}

# First Stage: Calculating mean and percent change
percent_change_summary <- mydata %>%
  filter(Exercise != "Bent Row") %>%
  group_by(Measure, Exercise) %>%
  summarise(
    PRE_Mean = mean(PRE, na.rm = TRUE),
    MID_Mean = mean(MID, na.rm = TRUE),
    POST_Mean = mean(POST, na.rm = TRUE),
    percent_change = list(calculate_percent_change(PRE_Mean, MID_Mean, POST_Mean)),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  unnest(percent_change)

percent_change_by_upperlower <- mydata %>%
  mutate(Exercise_Type = ifelse(Exercise %in% upper_exercises, "Upper", "Lower")) %>%
  group_by(Measure, Exercise_Type) %>%
  summarise(
    PRE_Mean = mean(PRE, na.rm = TRUE),
    MID_Mean = mean(MID, na.rm = TRUE),
    POST_Mean = mean(POST, na.rm = TRUE),
    percent_change = list(calculate_percent_change(PRE_Mean, MID_Mean, POST_Mean)),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  unnest(percent_change)

percent_change_per_subject_upperlower <- mydata %>%
  mutate(Exercise_Type = ifelse(Exercise %in% upper_exercises, "Upper", "Lower")) %>%
  group_by(Subject, Measure, Exercise_Type) %>%  # Include Subject here
  summarise(
    PRE = mean(PRE, na.rm = TRUE),
    MID = mean(MID, na.rm = TRUE),
    POST = mean(POST, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(percent_change = list(calculate_percent_change(PRE, MID, POST))) %>%
  unnest(percent_change)

percent_change_summary_with_pvalues_upperlower <- percent_change_per_subject_upperlower %>%
  # filter(Exercise != "Bent Row") %>%
  group_by(Measure, Exercise_Type) %>%
  summarise(
    p_val_pre_mid = t.test(PRE_to_MID, MID_to_POST, paired = TRUE, na.rm = TRUE)$p.value,
    p_val_pre_post = t.test(PRE_to_MID, PRE_to_POST, paired = TRUE, na.rm = TRUE)$p.value,
    p_val_mid_post = t.test(MID_to_POST, PRE_to_POST, paired = TRUE, na.rm = TRUE)$p.value,
    PRE_Mean_SD = sprintf("%.2f±%.2f", mean(PRE_to_MID, na.rm = TRUE), sd(PRE_to_MID, na.rm = TRUE)),
    MID_Mean_SD = sprintf("%.2f±%.2f", mean(MID_to_POST, na.rm = TRUE), sd(MID_to_POST, na.rm = TRUE)),
    POST_Mean_SD = sprintf("%.2f±%.2f", mean(PRE_to_POST, na.rm = TRUE), sd(PRE_to_POST, na.rm = TRUE)),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

percent_change_per_subject <- mydata %>%
  filter(Exercise != "Bent Row") %>%
  group_by(Subject, Measure, Exercise) %>%  # Include Subject here
  summarise(
    PRE = mean(PRE, na.rm = TRUE),
    MID = mean(MID, na.rm = TRUE),
    POST = mean(POST, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(percent_change = list(calculate_percent_change(PRE, MID, POST))) %>%
  unnest(percent_change)

min_max_values <- percent_change_per_subject %>%
  group_by(Measure, Exercise) %>%
  summarise(
    Min_PRE_to_MID = min(PRE_to_MID, na.rm = TRUE),
    Max_PRE_to_MID = max(PRE_to_MID, na.rm = TRUE),
    Min_MID_to_POST = min(MID_to_POST, na.rm = TRUE),
    Max_MID_to_POST = max(MID_to_POST, na.rm = TRUE),
    Min_PRE_to_POST = min(PRE_to_POST, na.rm = TRUE),
    Max_PRE_to_POST = max(PRE_to_POST, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
# Second Stage: Calculating p-values and Mean±SD
percent_change_summary_with_pvalues <- percent_change_per_subject %>%
  filter(Exercise != "Bent Row") %>%
  group_by(Measure, Exercise) %>%
  summarise(
    p_val_pre_mid = t.test(PRE_to_MID, MID_to_POST, paired = TRUE, na.rm = TRUE)$p.value,
    p_val_pre_post = t.test(PRE_to_MID, PRE_to_POST, paired = TRUE, na.rm = TRUE)$p.value,
    p_val_mid_post = t.test(MID_to_POST, PRE_to_POST, paired = TRUE, na.rm = TRUE)$p.value,
    PRE_Mean_SD = sprintf("%.2f±%.2f", mean(PRE_to_MID, na.rm = TRUE), sd(PRE_to_MID, na.rm = TRUE)),
    MID_Mean_SD = sprintf("%.2f±%.2f", mean(MID_to_POST, na.rm = TRUE), sd(MID_to_POST, na.rm = TRUE)),
    POST_Mean_SD = sprintf("%.2f±%.2f", mean(PRE_to_POST, na.rm = TRUE), sd(PRE_to_POST, na.rm = TRUE)),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
# Merge the tables using left_join
merged_summary <- left_join(percent_change_summary, percent_change_summary_with_pvalues,
                            by = c("Measure", "Exercise"))

file_name <- paste0(output_directory, "/", date, "Strength_percent_change_summary.xlsx")
write_xlsx(merged_summary, file_name)
file_name <- paste0(output_directory, "/", date, "_per_subject_Strength_percent_change_summary.xlsx")
write_xlsx(percent_change_per_subject, file_name)

# First Stage: Calculating mean and percent change
percent_change_summary_TvsU <- mydata %>%
  filter(Exercise != "Bent Row") %>%
  group_by(Measure, Exercise, TrainingStatus) %>%
  summarise(
    PRE_Mean = mean(PRE, na.rm = TRUE),
    MID_Mean = mean(MID, na.rm = TRUE),
    POST_Mean = mean(POST, na.rm = TRUE),
    percent_change = list(calculate_percent_change(PRE_Mean, MID_Mean, POST_Mean)),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  unnest(percent_change)

percent_change_by_upperlower <- mydata %>%
  mutate(Exercise_Type = ifelse(Exercise %in% upper_exercises, "Upper", "Lower")) %>%
  group_by(Measure, Exercise_Type, TrainingStatus) %>%
  summarise(
    PRE_Mean = mean(PRE, na.rm = TRUE),
    MID_Mean = mean(MID, na.rm = TRUE),
    POST_Mean = mean(POST, na.rm = TRUE),
    percent_change = list(calculate_percent_change(PRE_Mean, MID_Mean, POST_Mean)),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  unnest(percent_change)

percent_change_per_subject_upperlower <- mydata %>%
  mutate(Exercise_Type = ifelse(Exercise %in% upper_exercises, "Upper", "Lower")) %>%
  group_by(Subject, Measure, Exercise_Type, TrainingStatus) %>%  # Include Subject here
  summarise(
    PRE = mean(PRE, na.rm = TRUE),
    MID = mean(MID, na.rm = TRUE),
    POST = mean(POST, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(percent_change = list(calculate_percent_change(PRE, MID, POST))) %>%
  unnest(percent_change)

percent_change_summary_with_pvalues_upperlower_TvsU <- percent_change_per_subject_upperlower %>%
  # filter(Exercise != "Bent Row") %>%
  group_by(Measure, Exercise_Type, TrainingStatus) %>%
  summarise(
    p_val_pre_mid = t.test(PRE_to_MID, MID_to_POST, paired = TRUE, na.rm = TRUE)$p.value,
    p_val_pre_post = t.test(PRE_to_MID, PRE_to_POST, paired = TRUE, na.rm = TRUE)$p.value,
    p_val_mid_post = t.test(MID_to_POST, PRE_to_POST, paired = TRUE, na.rm = TRUE)$p.value,
    PRE_Mean_SD = sprintf("%.2f±%.2f", mean(PRE_to_MID, na.rm = TRUE), sd(PRE_to_MID, na.rm = TRUE)),
    MID_Mean_SD = sprintf("%.2f±%.2f", mean(MID_to_POST, na.rm = TRUE), sd(MID_to_POST, na.rm = TRUE)),
    POST_Mean_SD = sprintf("%.2f±%.2f", mean(PRE_to_POST, na.rm = TRUE), sd(PRE_to_POST, na.rm = TRUE)),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

percent_change_per_subject_TvsU <- mydata %>%
  filter(Exercise != "Bent Row") %>%
  group_by(Subject, Measure, Exercise, TrainingStatus) %>%  # Include Subject here
  summarise(
    PRE = mean(PRE, na.rm = TRUE),
    MID = mean(MID, na.rm = TRUE),
    POST = mean(POST, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(percent_change = list(calculate_percent_change(PRE, MID, POST))) %>%
  unnest(percent_change)

min_max_values <- percent_change_per_subject_TvsU %>%
  group_by(Measure, Exercise, TrainingStatus) %>%
  summarise(
    Min_PRE_to_MID = min(PRE_to_MID, na.rm = TRUE),
    Max_PRE_to_MID = max(PRE_to_MID, na.rm = TRUE),
    Min_MID_to_POST = min(MID_to_POST, na.rm = TRUE),
    Max_MID_to_POST = max(MID_to_POST, na.rm = TRUE),
    Min_PRE_to_POST = min(PRE_to_POST, na.rm = TRUE),
    Max_PRE_to_POST = max(PRE_to_POST, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
# Second Stage: Calculating p-values and Mean±SD
percent_change_summary_with_pvalues_TvsU <- percent_change_per_subject_TvsU %>%
  filter(Exercise != "Bent Row") %>%
  group_by(Measure, Exercise, TrainingStatus) %>%
  summarise(
    p_val_pre_mid = t.test(PRE_to_MID, MID_to_POST, paired = TRUE, na.rm = TRUE)$p.value,
    p_val_pre_post = t.test(PRE_to_MID, PRE_to_POST, paired = TRUE, na.rm = TRUE)$p.value,
    p_val_mid_post = t.test(MID_to_POST, PRE_to_POST, paired = TRUE, na.rm = TRUE)$p.value,
    PRE_Mean_SD = sprintf("%.2f±%.2f", mean(PRE_to_MID, na.rm = TRUE), sd(PRE_to_MID, na.rm = TRUE)),
    MID_Mean_SD = sprintf("%.2f±%.2f", mean(MID_to_POST, na.rm = TRUE), sd(MID_to_POST, na.rm = TRUE)),
    POST_Mean_SD = sprintf("%.2f±%.2f", mean(PRE_to_POST, na.rm = TRUE), sd(PRE_to_POST, na.rm = TRUE)),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
# Merge the tables using left_join
merged_summary <- left_join(percent_change_summary_TvsU, percent_change_summary_with_pvalues_TvsU,
                            by = c("Measure", "Exercise", "TrainingStatus"))

pivot_summary <- percent_change_per_subject_TvsU %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(PRE_to_MID, MID_to_POST, PRE_to_POST),
               names_to = "Timepoint", values_to = "Value")

# Now perform the comparison
comparison_results <- pivot_summary %>%
  group_by(Measure, Exercise, Timepoint) %>%
  do(tidy(t.test(Value ~ TrainingStatus, data = .))) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  select(Measure, Exercise, Timepoint, p.value)
# View the results
TvsU <- filter(comparison_results, p.value < 0.1)
kable(TvsU, digits = 3, caption = "T-test results for TrainingStatus")

file_name <- paste0(output_directory, "/", date, "TvsU_percent_change_summary.xlsx")
write_xlsx(merged_summary, file_name)

file_name <- paste0(output_directory, "/", date, "_TvsU_per_subject_Strength_percent_change_summary.xlsx")
write_xlsx(percent_change_per_subject, file_name)
```

## Analysis of Percent Changes in Strength Measures

The percent change analysis provides valuable insights into the magnitude and timing of strength adaptations across different exercises and between trained and untrained participants.

### Overall Percent Changes

Across all participants, substantial improvements in strength were observed:

- **10RM measures** showed greater overall percent increases (25.7-45.1%) compared to **1RM measures** (17.9-42.1%)
- **Deadlift** exhibited the largest percent increases for both 10RM (45.1%) and 1RM (42.1%) measures
- **Bench Press** showed the smallest percent increases for both 10RM (25.7%) and 1RM (17.9%) measures
- For most exercises, the **PRE to MID** phase showed larger percent increases than the **MID to POST** phase, suggesting a slight diminishing rate of adaptation over time

### Trained vs. Untrained Comparisons

The data revealed interesting patterns when comparing trained vs. untrained individuals:

1. **Lower body exercises:**
   - Trained individuals showed substantially greater PRE to MID improvements in 1RM Leg Press compared to untrained (32.9% vs. 11.3%)
   - For 10RM lower body exercises, trained and untrained individuals showed remarkably similar percent improvements (24.6% vs. 24.8%)

2. **Upper body exercises:**
   - Trained individuals demonstrated larger improvements in upper body exercises for both 10RM (24.9% vs. 19.4%) and 1RM (20.6% vs. 11.3%)
   - The 1RM Lat Pull showed a pronounced difference between trained and untrained (17.1% vs. 9.5% for PRE to MID, and 32.9% vs. 17.5% overall)

3. **Exercise-specific differences:**
   - 10RM Seated Row showed the largest disparity between trained and untrained for overall improvement (51.5% vs. 32.2%)
   - 1RM Leg Press showed significantly different adaptation patterns, with trained individuals gaining more in the early phase, while untrained individuals showed greater improvements in the later phase

### Individual Variability

The minimum and maximum values reveal considerable individual variability in responses:

- Some individuals showed no improvement (0%) during certain phases, while others demonstrated increases exceeding 100% in extreme cases
- The range of responses was particularly large for 10RM measures, with maximum values as high as 133.3% (10RM Seated Row in untrained individuals)
- Even within trained individuals, the range of responses was substantial, with maximum values for PRE to POST changes ranging from 29.6% to 118.5%

### Statistical Significance

The p-values for percent changes highlight:

- Most exercises showed statistically significant improvements from PRE to POST in both groups
- The significance of MID to POST changes varied more across exercises, suggesting different patterns of continued adaptation
- Significant p-values were more consistent in trained individuals for upper body exercises

### Practical Implications

These findings have several practical implications for resistance training program design:

1. Both trained and untrained individuals can achieve substantial strength gains, but the pattern and magnitude may differ
2. Upper body exercises may require different programming approaches for trained vs. untrained individuals
3. Lower body adaptations appear more consistent between training status groups
4. Individual response variability suggests the importance of personalized monitoring and adjustment of training programs

The data challenges the common assumption that untrained individuals will always show greater relative improvements, particularly for upper body exercises where trained individuals often demonstrated larger percent gains.

```{r Percent Change1, echo=FALSE}
kable(percent_change_summary, caption = "Percent change summary", digits = 3)
kable(percent_change_summary_with_pvalues, caption = "Percent change summary with p-values", digits = 3)
kable(percent_change_by_upperlower, caption = "Percent change by upper and lower exercises", digits = 3)
kable(percent_change_summary_TvsU, caption = "Percent change summary for TrainingStatus", digits = 3)
kable(percent_change_summary_with_pvalues_TvsU, caption = "Percent change summary with p-values for TrainingStatus", digits = 3)
kable(percent_change_summary_with_pvalues_upperlower_TvsU, caption = "Percent change summary with p-values for upper and lower exercises for TrainingStatus", digits = 3)
kable(min_max_values, caption = "Min and Max values for percent change", digits = 3)
```



```{r Plotting 10rm, echo=FALSE, warnings=FALSE, message=FALSE}
# Read in the Excel file and perform all transformations in a single pipeline
mydata <- read_excel("MRP.xlsx", sheet = "Strength", col_names = TRUE) %>%
  select(1:3, 7:9) %>%
  rename(Subject = 1, Measure = 2, Exercise = 3, PRE = 4, MID = 5, POST = 6) %>%
  filter(!(Subject %in% c("SUB 5", "SUB 13"))) %>%
  mutate(
    PRE = as.numeric(PRE),
    MID = as.numeric(MID),
    POST = as.numeric(POST)
  )

# Create a new dataframe with 1RM data only, filtering out "Bent Over Row" and removing the Measure column
mydata_1rm <- mydata %>%
  filter(Exercise != "Bent Over Row", str_detect(Measure, "1RM")) %>%
  select(-Measure)
mydata_1rm$Exercise <- dplyr::recode(mydata_1rm$Exercise, !!!replacements)
# Create a new dataframe with 10RM data only, filtering out "Bent Over Row" and removing the Measure column
mydata_10rm <- mydata %>%
  filter(Exercise != "Bent Over Row", str_detect(Measure, "10RM")) %>%
  select(-Measure)
mydata_10rm$Exercise <- dplyr::recode(mydata_10rm$Exercise, !!!replacements)

# Calculate means and standard deviations for each exercise and time point
mydata_10rm_summary <- mydata_10rm %>%
  group_by(Exercise) %>%
  summarise(across(
    c(PRE, MID, POST),
    list(Mean = mean, SD = sd), # Capitalize the Mean here
    .names = "{.col}_{.fn}")  )

# Reshape the data into a longer format
mydata_10rm_summary_long <- mydata_10rm_summary %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(PRE_Mean:POST_SD), # Update column names to match the new pattern
    names_to = c("Time", ".value"),
    names_pattern = "(PRE|MID|POST)_(.*)"
  )

#reorder so that the plot displays in order of magnitude and pre mid post.
mydata_10rm_summary_long <- mydata_10rm_summary_long %>%
  mutate(Exercise = reorder(Exercise, -Mean))
mydata_10rm_summary_long$Time <- factor(mydata_10rm_summary_long$Time, levels = c("PRE", "MID", "POST"))


# create a vector indicating which bars are significantly different
sig_diff_rm10s <- c(F, T, T, F, T, T, F, T, T, F, T, T, F, T, T, F, T, T)

# plot with markers for significant differences COLOR
rm10splot <- ggplot(mydata_10rm_summary_long, aes(x = Exercise, y = Mean, fill = Time)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = Mean - SD, ymax = Mean + SD),
                position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), width = 0.2) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), width = 0.9) +
  geom_text(aes(y = Mean + SD + 2, label = ifelse(sig_diff_rm10s, "*", "")),
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), size = 10) +
  labs(x = "", y = "10RM (kg)", fill = "Time") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(dark_blue_color, light_blue_color, third_color)) +  # Apply custom fill colors
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 165),
                     breaks = seq(0, 160, 20),
                     minor_breaks = seq(0, 160, 10),
                     expand = c(0, 0)) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        legend.position = c(0.85, 0.8),
        panel.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
        panel.border = element_blank(),
        text = element_text(size = 12),          # Set font size for general text
        axis.title = element_text(size = 12, color = "black"),    # Set font size for axis titles
        axis.text = element_text(size = 11, color = "black"),     # Set font size for axis text
        legend.title = element_text(size = 12),  # Set font size for legend title
        legend.text = element_text(size = 12))   # Set font size for legend text))

# Save the plot to a png file at 1200 dpi
file_name_plot <- paste0(output_directory, "/", date, "_COLOR_10rm_plot_sig.png")
ggsave(file_name_plot, plot = rm10splot, width = 6, height = 4, dpi = 1200)
print(rm10splot)
```

```{r plotting 1rm, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
# Calculate means and standard deviations for each exercise and time point
mydata_1rm_summary <- mydata_1rm %>%
  group_by(Exercise) %>%
  summarise(across(
    c(PRE, MID, POST),
    list(Mean = mean, SD = sd), # Capitalize the Mean here
    .names = "{.col}_{.fn}")
  )
# Reshape the data into a longer format
mydata_1rm_summary_long <- mydata_1rm_summary %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(PRE_Mean:POST_SD), # Update column names to match the new pattern
    names_to = c("Time", ".value"),
    names_pattern = "(PRE|MID|POST)_(.*)"
  )
# Reorder so that the plot displays in order of magnitude and PRE, MID, POST.
mydata_1rm_summary_long <- mydata_1rm_summary_long %>%
  mutate(Exercise = reorder(Exercise, -Mean))
mydata_1rm_summary_long$Time <- factor(mydata_1rm_summary_long$Time, levels = c("PRE", "MID", "POST"))

# create a vector indicating which bars are significantly different
sig_diff_rm1s <- c(F, T, T, F, T, T, F, T, T, F, T, T, F, T, T, F, T, T)
  # plot with markers for significant differences
  rm1splot <- ggplot(mydata_1rm_summary_long, aes(x = Exercise, y = Mean, fill = Time)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = Mean - SD, ymax = Mean + SD),
                position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), width = 0.2) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), width = 0.9) +
  geom_text(aes(y = Mean + SD + 2, label = ifelse(sig_diff_rm1s, "*", "")),
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), size = 10) +
  labs(x = "", y = "1RM (kg)", fill = "Time") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(dark_blue_color, light_blue_color, third_color)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 165),
                     breaks = seq(0, 160, 20),
                     minor_breaks = seq(0, 160, 10),
                     expand = c(0, 0)) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        legend.position = c(0.85, 0.8),
        panel.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
        plot.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
        panel.border = element_blank(),
        text = element_text(size = 12),          # Set font size for general text
        axis.title = element_text(size = 12, color= "black"),    # Set font size for axis titles
        axis.text = element_text(size = 11, color = "black"),     # Set font size for axis text
        legend.title = element_text(size = 12),  # Set font size for legend title
        legend.text = element_text(size = 12))   # Set font size for legend text))

# Save the plot to a png file at 1200 dpi
file_name_plot <- paste0(output_directory, "/", date, "_COLOR_1rm_plot_sig.png")
ggsave(file_name_plot, plot = rm1splot, width = 6, height = 4, dpi = 1200)
print(rm1splot)
```



```{r plotting, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
# Calculate mean and standard deviation for the specified columns
summary_data <- percent_change_per_subject_TvsU %>%
  group_by(Measure, TrainingStatus) %>%
  summarize(
    mean_PRE_to_MID = mean(PRE_to_MID),
    sd_PRE_to_MID = sd(PRE_to_MID),
    mean_MID_to_POST = mean(MID_to_POST),
    sd_MID_to_POST = sd(MID_to_POST),
    mean_PRE_to_POST = mean(PRE_to_POST),
    sd_PRE_to_POST = sd(PRE_to_POST)
  )
# Subset rows with "1RM" in the Measure column and retain TrainingStatus
measures_1RM <- summary_data %>%
  filter(str_detect(Measure, "1RM")) %>%
  mutate(Measure = str_replace(Measure, "_1RM", "")) # Remove "_1RM" suffix
# Create a new column for asterisks
measures_1RM$Asterisk <- ifelse((measures_1RM$Measure == "OHP" & measures_1RM$TrainingStatus == "trained") |
                                  (measures_1RM$Measure == "Seated Row" & measures_1RM$TrainingStatus == "untrained"), "", "*")
# Reorder the factor levels for the 'TrainingStatus' column
measures_1RM$TrainingStatus <- factor(measures_1RM$TrainingStatus, levels = c("untrained", "trained"))

# Reorder factor levels for Exercise variable
exercise_order <- c("Leg Press", "Deadlift", "Seated Row", "Lat Pull", "Bench Press", "OHP")
measures_1RM$Measure <- factor(measures_1RM$Measure, levels = exercise_order)

# Plot with markers for significant differences
plot_1RM_with_asterisks <- ggplot(measures_1RM, aes(x = Measure, y = mean_PRE_to_POST, fill = TrainingStatus)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_PRE_to_POST - sd_PRE_to_POST, ymax = mean_PRE_to_POST + sd_PRE_to_POST),
                position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), width = 0.2) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), width = 0.9) +
  geom_text(aes(y = mean_PRE_to_POST + sd_PRE_to_POST + 2, label = Asterisk), size = 10, color = "black",
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.9)) +
  labs(x = "", y = "1RM %Δ", fill = "Training Status") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(dark_blue_color, light_blue_color)) +  # Apply custom fill colors
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 130),
                     breaks = seq(0, 120, 20),
                     minor_breaks = seq(0, 120, 10),
                     expand = c(0, 0)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        legend.position = c(0.85, 0.8),
        panel.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
        panel.border = element_blank(),
        text = element_text(size = 12),          # Set font size for general text
        axis.title = element_text(size = 12, color = "black"),    # Set font size for axis titles
        axis.text = element_text(size = 11, color = "black"),     # Set font size for axis text
        legend.title = element_text(size = 12),  # Set font size for legend title
        legend.text = element_text(size = 12))   # Set font size for legend text))

# Subset rows with "10RM" in the Measure column and retain TrainingStatus
measures_10RM <- summary_data %>%
  filter(str_detect(Measure, "10RM")) %>%
  mutate(Measure = str_replace(Measure, "_10RM", "")) # Remove "_10RM" suffix
# Reorder the factor levels for the 'TrainingStatus' column
measures_10RM$TrainingStatus <- factor(measures_1RM$TrainingStatus, levels = c("untrained", "trained"))

# Create a new column for asterisks
measures_10RM$Asterisk <- ifelse((measures_1RM$Measure == "Bench Press" & measures_1RM$TrainingStatus == "trained") |
                                  (measures_1RM$Measure == "" & measures_1RM$TrainingStatus == "untrained"), "", "*")
# Reorder factor levels for Exercise variable
exercise_order <- c("Leg Press", "Deadlift", "Seated Row", "Lat Pull", "Bench Press",  "OHP")
measures_10RM$Measure <- factor(measures_10RM$Measure, levels = exercise_order)

# Plot with markers for significant differences
plot_10RM_with_asterisks <- ggplot(measures_10RM, aes(x = Measure, y = mean_PRE_to_POST, fill = TrainingStatus)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_PRE_to_POST - sd_PRE_to_POST, ymax = mean_PRE_to_POST + sd_PRE_to_POST),
                position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), width = 0.2) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), width = 0.9) +
  geom_text(aes(y = mean_PRE_to_POST + sd_PRE_to_POST + 2, label = Asterisk), size = 10, color = "black",
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.9)) +
  labs(x = "", y = "10RM %Δ", fill = "Training Status") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(dark_blue_color, light_blue_color)) +  # Apply custom fill colors
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 130),
                     breaks = seq(0, 120, 20),
                     minor_breaks = seq(0, 120, 10),
                     expand = c(0, 0)) +
  theme(panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        legend.position = c(0.85, 0.8),
        panel.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
        panel.border = element_blank(),
        text = element_text(size = 12),          # Set font size for general text
        axis.title = element_text(size = 12, color = "black"),    # Set font size for axis titles
        axis.text = element_text(size = 11, color = "black"),     # Set font size for axis text
        legend.title = element_text(size = 12),  # Set font size for legend title
        legend.text = element_text(size = 12))   # Set font size for legend text))
```

![10RM Plot](02212024_output/02212024_SIG_10RM_plot_TvsU.png)
![1RM Plot ](02212024_output/02212024_SIG_1RM_plot_TvsU.png)

```{r Plot 1RM and 10RM faceted by TrainingStatus, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
# Read in the Excel file and perform all transformations in a single pipeline
mydata <- read_excel("MRP.xlsx", sheet = "Strength", col_names = TRUE) %>%
 dplyr::select(1:3, 7:9) %>%
  rename(Subject = 1, Measure = 2, Exercise = 3, PRE = 4, MID = 5, POST = 6) %>%
  filter(!(Subject %in% c("SUB 5", "SUB 13"))) %>%
  mutate(
    PRE = as.numeric(PRE),
    MID = as.numeric(MID),
    POST = as.numeric(POST)
  )
# Apply the replacements to the Exercise column
mydata$Exercise <- dplyr::recode(mydata$Exercise, !!!replacements)

# Add a new column "TrainingStatus" based on the subjects
mydata <- mydata %>%
  mutate(TrainingStatus = ifelse(Subject %in% trained_subjects, "trained", "untrained"))
# Remove BentRow entries from mydata
mydata <- mydata[mydata$Measure != "BentRow_1RM" & mydata$Measure != "BentRow_10RM", ]
mydata <- mydata %>%
  filter(Exercise != "Bent Row")

#Filter 10rm and 1rm to new tables
# Function to summarize and reshape the data
summarize_and_reshape <- function(data) {
  summary <- data %>%
    group_by(Exercise) %>%
    summarise(across(
      c(PRE, MID, POST),
      list(Mean = mean, SD = sd),
      .names = "{.col}_{.fn}")
    )

  summary_long <- summary %>%
    pivot_longer(
      cols = c(PRE_Mean:POST_SD),
      names_to = c("Time", ".value"),
      names_pattern = "(PRE|MID|POST)_(.*)"
    ) %>%
    mutate(Exercise = reorder(Exercise, -Mean),
           Time = factor(Time, levels = c("PRE", "MID", "POST")))
  return(summary_long)
}
# Filter 10rm and 1rm to new tables and apply the function
mydata_1rm_T <- mydata %>% filter(Measure == "1RM" & TrainingStatus == "trained") %>% summarize_and_reshape()
mydata_1rm_U <- mydata %>% filter(Measure == "1RM" & TrainingStatus == "untrained") %>% summarize_and_reshape()
mydata_10rm_T <- mydata %>% filter(Measure == "10RM" & TrainingStatus == "trained") %>% summarize_and_reshape()
mydata_10rm_U <- mydata %>% filter(Measure == "10RM" & TrainingStatus == "untrained") %>% summarize_and_reshape()

# create a vector indicating which bars are significantly different
sig_diff_1rm_T <- c(F,F,T,F,T,T,F,T,T,F,T,T,F,F,F,F,T,T)
#(BP, DL, LAT, LP ,OHP,SR)
# plot with markers for significant differences
rm1_T_plot <-
  ggplot(mydata_1rm_T, aes(x = Exercise, y = Mean, fill = Time)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = Mean - SD, ymax = Mean + SD),
                position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), width = 0.2) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), width = 0.9) +
  geom_text(aes(y = Mean + SD + 2, label =ifelse(sig_diff_1rm_T, "*", "")),
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), size = 10) +
  labs(x = "Trained", y = "1RM (kg)", fill = "Time") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(dark_blue_color, light_blue_color, third_color)) +

  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 165),
                     breaks = seq(0, 160, 20),
                     minor_breaks = seq(0, 160, 10),
                     expand = c(0, 0)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        legend.position = c(0.85, 0.8),
        panel.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
        plot.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
        panel.border = element_blank())

# Save the plot to a png file at 1200 dpi
file_name_plot <- paste0(output_directory, "/", date, "1rm_T_plot_sig.png")
ggsave(file_name_plot, plot = rm1_T_plot, width = 6, height = 4, dpi = 1200)

sig_diff_1rm_U <- c(F,T,T,F,T,T,F,T,T,F,F,T,F,F,T,F,F,F)
#(BP, DL, LAT, LP ,OHP,SR)
rm1_U_plot<-
ggplot(mydata_1rm_U, aes(x = Exercise, y = Mean, fill = Time)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = Mean - SD, ymax = Mean + SD),
                position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), width = 0.2) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), width = 0.9) +
  geom_text(aes(y = Mean + SD + 2, label = ifelse(sig_diff_1rm_U, "*", "")),
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), size = 10) +
  labs(x = "Untrained", y = "1RM (kg)", fill = "Time") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(dark_blue_color, light_blue_color, third_color)) +

  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 165),
                     breaks = seq(0, 160, 20),
                     minor_breaks = seq(0, 160, 10),
                     expand = c(0, 0)) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        legend.position = c(0.85, 0.8),
        panel.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
        plot.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
        panel.border = element_blank())

file_name_plot <- paste0(output_directory, "/", date, "1rm_U_plot_sig.png")
ggsave(file_name_plot, plot = rm1_U_plot, width = 6, height = 4, dpi = 1200)

# Combine the plots using plot_grid and include labels
combined_plot <- plot_grid(rm1_U_plot, rm1_T_plot, nrow = 1)
print(combined_plot)

file_name_plot <- paste0(output_directory, "/", date, "1rm_TvsU_plot_sig.png")
ggsave(file_name_plot, plot = combined_plot, width = 6, height = 4, dpi = 1200)

# Reorder factor levels for Exercise variable
exercise_order <- c("Leg Press", "Deadlift", "Seated Row", "Lat Pull", "Bench Press",  "OHP")
mydata_10rm_T$Exercise <- factor(mydata_10rm_T$Exercise, levels = exercise_order)
# create a vector indicating which bars are significantly different
sig_diff_10rm_T <- c(F,F,F,F,T,T,F,F,T,F,T,T,F,F,T,F,T,T)
#(BP, DL, LAT, LP ,OHP,SR)
# plot with markers for significant differences
rm10_T_plot <-
  ggplot(mydata_10rm_T, aes(x = Exercise, y = Mean, fill = Time)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = Mean - SD, ymax = Mean + SD),
                position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), width = 0.2) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), width = 0.9) +
  geom_text(aes(y = Mean + SD + 2, label =ifelse(sig_diff_10rm_T, "*", "")),
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), size = 10) +
  labs(x = "Trained", y = "10RM (kg)", fill = "Time") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(dark_blue_color, light_blue_color, third_color)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 165),
                     breaks = seq(0, 160, 20),
                     minor_breaks = seq(0, 160, 10),
                     expand = c(0, 0)) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        legend.position = c(0.85, 0.8),
        panel.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
        plot.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
        panel.border = element_blank())
# Save the plot to a png file at 1200 dpi
file_name_plot <- paste0(output_directory, "/", date, "10rm_T_plot_sig.png")
ggsave(file_name_plot, plot = rm10_T_plot, width = 6, height = 4, dpi = 1200)

sig_diff_10rm_U <- c(F,T,T,F,T,T,F,T,T,F,T,T,F,F,T,F,T,T)
#(BP, DL, LAT, LP ,OHP,SR)
rm10_U_plot<-
  ggplot(mydata_10rm_U, aes(x = Exercise, y = Mean, fill = Time)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = Mean - SD, ymax = Mean + SD),
                position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), width = 0.2) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), width = 0.9) +
  geom_text(aes(y = Mean + SD + 2, label = ifelse(sig_diff_10rm_U, "*", "")),
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), size = 10) +
  labs(x = "Untrained", y = "10RM (kg)", fill = "Time") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(dark_blue_color, light_blue_color, third_color)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 165),
                     breaks = seq(0, 160, 20),
                     minor_breaks = seq(0, 160, 10),
                     expand = c(0, 0)) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        legend.position = c(0.85, 0.8),
        panel.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
        plot.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
        panel.border = element_blank())

file_name_plot <- paste0(output_directory, "/", date, "10rm_U_plot_sig.png")
ggsave(file_name_plot, plot = rm10_U_plot, width = 6, height = 4, dpi = 1200)

# Combine the plots using plot_grid and include labels
combined_plot <- plot_grid(rm10_U_plot, rm10_T_plot, nrow = 1)

# Display the combined plot
print(combined_plot)
file_name_plot <- paste0(output_directory, "/", date, "10rm_TvsU_plot_sig.png")
ggsave(file_name_plot, plot = combined_plot, width = 6, height = 4, dpi = 1200)
```

# VO2 and other variables

```{r VO2 and extra aov analysis  , echo=FALSE}
# read in the data from the Excel file
mydata <- read_excel("MRP.xlsx", sheet = "vo2data", col_names = TRUE)

# Add a new column "TrainingStatus" based on the subjects
mydata <- mydata %>%
  mutate(TrainingStatus = ifelse(Subject %in% trained_subjects, "trained", "untrained"))

# Create a list of measures
measures <- c("VO2Max", "VO2Peak", "Body fat (%)", "Weight (Kg)", "Vertical Jump","6.5km*hr-1", "16km*hr-1",
              "12km*hr-1")
# Create the file name by appending the date to "anova_results.txt"
file_name <- paste0(output_directory, "/", date, "anova_results.txt")

# Open the file connection
fileConn <- file(file_name, "w")
# Loop through each measure and perform ANOVA
for (measure in measures) {
  # Subset the data for the current measure
  measure_data <- subset(mydata, Measure == measure)
  # Convert the data to long format
  long_data <- pivot_longer(measure_data, cols = c(PRE, MID, POST), names_to = "Timepoint", values_to = "Value")
  # Fit aov model with long format data
  aov_result <- aov(Value ~ Timepoint + Error(Subject), data = long_data)
  # Print ANOVA table with measure name
  cat("Measure:", measure, "\n")
  print(summary(aov_result))
  # Write ANOVA table with measure name to file
  cat("Measure:", measure, "\n", file = fileConn)
  writeLines(capture.output(print(summary(aov_result))), fileConn)
}
# Close the file connection
close(fileConn)

mydata_T <- mydata[mydata$Subject %in% trained_subjects, ]
# Create the file name by appending the date to "anova_results.txt"
file_name <- paste0(output_directory, "/", date, "trained_anova_results.txt", sep = "_")

# Open the file connection
fileConn <- file(file_name, "w")
# Loop through each measure and perform ANOVA
for (measure in measures) {
  # Subset the data for the current measure
  measure_data <- subset(mydata_T, Measure == measure)
  # Convert the data to long format
  long_data <- pivot_longer(measure_data, cols = c(PRE, MID, POST), names_to = "Timepoint", values_to = "Value")
  # Fit aov model with long format data
  aov_result <- aov(Value ~ Timepoint + Error(Subject), data = long_data)
  # Write ANOVA table with measure name to file
  cat("Measure:", measure, "\n", file = fileConn)
  writeLines(capture.output(print(summary(aov_result))), fileConn)
}
# Close the file connection
close(fileConn)

# Create a list of measures
measures <- c("VO2Max", "VO2Peak", "Body fat (%)", "Weight (Kg)", "Vertical Jump","6.5km*hr-1",
              #"16km*hr-1",
              "12km*hr-1")
mydata_U <- mydata[mydata$Subject %in% untrained_subjects, ]
# Create the file name by appending the date to "anova_results.txt"
file_name <- paste0(output_directory, "/", date, "untrained_anova_results.txt", sep = "_")

# Open the file connection
fileConn <- file(file_name, "w")
# Loop through each measure and perform ANOVA
for (measure in measures) {
  # Subset the data for the current measure
  measure_data <- subset(mydata_U, Measure == measure)
  # Convert the data to long format
  long_data <- pivot_longer(measure_data, cols = c(PRE, MID, POST), names_to = "Timepoint", values_to = "Value")
  # Fit aov model with long format data
  aov_result <- aov(Value ~ Timepoint + Error(Subject), data = long_data)
  # Write ANOVA table with measure name to file
  cat("Measure:", measure, "\n", file = fileConn)
  writeLines(capture.output(print(summary(aov_result))), fileConn)
}
# Close the file connection
close(fileConn)
```

### Normality and homogeneity tests

```{r VO2 and other variables summary, echo=FALSE}
mydata_reshaped <- mydata %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c("PRE", "MID", "POST"), names_to = "Timepoint", values_to = "Weight") %>%
  mutate(Timepoint = factor(Timepoint, levels = c("PRE", "MID", "POST")))

#Assessing Normality and homogeneity of variance
# Group your data by 'Measure_Exercise' and perform the Shapiro-Wilk test for each group
normality_tests <- mydata_reshaped %>%
  filter(Weight != 0) %>%
  group_by(Measure, Timepoint) %>%
  summarize(p_value = shapiro.test(Weight)$p.value, .groups = 'drop')
# Filter for sig non-normality
fnt <- filter(normality_tests, p_value < 0.05)
kable(fnt, digits = 3, caption = "Shapiro-Wilk test for normality group_by(Measure, Timepoint) ")

# Filter for sig non-normality
mydata_long2 <- mydata_reshaped %>%
  filter(Weight != 0) %>%
    filter(Measure %in% c("12km*hr-1", "YYIR"))

ggplot(mydata_long2, aes(x = Weight)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 5, fill = "lightblue", color = "black") +
  facet_wrap(~ Measure, scales = "free") +
  labs(x = "Value", y = "Frequency") +
  ggtitle("Histogram Facetted by Measure_Exercise") +
  theme_minimal()

# Group your data by 'Measure_Exercise' and perform the Shapiro-Wilk test for each group
normality_tests <- mydata_reshaped %>%
  filter(Weight != 0) %>%
  group_by(Measure, Timepoint, TrainingStatus) %>%
  summarize(p_value = shapiro.test(Weight)$p.value, .groups = 'drop')
# Filter for sig non-normality
fnt <- filter(normality_tests, p_value < 0.05)
kable(fnt, digits = 3, caption = "Shapiro-Wilk test for normality, group_by(Measure, Timepoint, TrainingStatus)")

# Group your data by 'Measure_Exercise' and perform the Shapiro-Wilk test for each group
normality_tests <- mydata_reshaped %>%
  filter(Weight != 0) %>%
  group_by(Measure, TrainingStatus) %>%
  summarize(p_value = shapiro.test(Weight)$p.value, .groups = 'drop')
# Filter for sig non-normality
fnt <- filter(normality_tests, p_value < 0.05)
kable(fnt, digits = 3, caption = "Shapiro-Wilk test for normality, group_by(Measure, TrainingStatus) ")

# Group your data by 'Measure_Exercise' and perform the Shapiro-Wilk test for each group
normality_tests <- mydata_reshaped %>%
  filter(Weight != 0) %>%
  group_by(Measure) %>%
  summarize(p_value = shapiro.test(Weight)$p.value, .groups = 'drop')
# Filter for sig non-normality
fnt <- filter(normality_tests, p_value < 0.05)
kable(fnt, digits = 3, caption = "Shapiro-Wilk test for normality, group_by(Measure) ")

```

### No violation of normality overall, at select timepoints, only YYIR and RE 12km*hr-1 are non-normal. Skew comes from trained subjects, so not surprising.

```{r VO2 and other variables homogeneity, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Perform Levene's Test for homogeneity of variances
leveneTest(Weight ~ Timepoint *Measure, data = mydata_reshaped)

# Group your data by 'Measure' and perform Levene's Test for each group
levene_results <- mydata_reshaped %>%
  group_by(Measure) %>%
  do(tidy(leveneTest(Weight ~ Timepoint, data = .)))

# Print results
kable(levene_results, digits = 3)
levene_results <- filter(levene_results, p.value < 0.05)

# Group your data by 'Measure' and perform Levene's Test for each group
levene_results <- mydata_reshaped %>%
  group_by(Measure, TrainingStatus) %>%
  do(tidy(leveneTest(Weight ~ Timepoint, data = .)))

# Print results
kable(levene_results, digits = 3)
levene_results <- filter(levene_results, p.value < 0.05)
```

#No sig differences in variance between timepoints for any measure.

```{r VO2 and other variables summary2, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Generate summary of mean and stdev grouped by measure. Additionally, output formatted mean±stdev for easy table
mydata_summary <- mydata %>%
  group_by(Measure) %>%
  summarize(
    PRE_Mean = mean(`PRE`, na.rm = TRUE),
    PRE_SD = sd(`PRE`, na.rm = TRUE),
    PRE_n = sum(!is.na(PRE)),
    MID_Mean = mean(`MID`, na.rm = TRUE),
    MID_SD = sd(`MID`, na.rm = TRUE),
    MID_n = sum(!is.na(MID)),
    POST_Mean = mean(`POST`, na.rm = TRUE),
    POST_SD = sd(`POST`, na.rm = TRUE),
    POST_n = sum(!is.na(POST)),
    PRE_Mean_SD = sprintf("%.2f±%.2f", PRE_Mean, PRE_SD),
    MID_Mean_SD = sprintf("%.2f±%.2f", MID_Mean, MID_SD),
    POST_Mean_SD = sprintf("%.2f±%.2f", POST_Mean, POST_SD)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    # Hedge's g for PRE to MID with bias correction
    hedges_g_pre_mid = ((MID_Mean - PRE_Mean) / 
                        sqrt((((PRE_n - 1) * PRE_SD^2) + ((MID_n - 1) * MID_SD^2)) / 
                             (PRE_n + MID_n - 2))) * 
                        (1 - (3 / (4 * (PRE_n + MID_n - 2) - 1))),
    
    # Hedge's g for PRE to POST with bias correction
    hedges_g_pre_post = ((POST_Mean - PRE_Mean) / 
                         sqrt((((PRE_n - 1) * PRE_SD^2) + ((POST_n - 1) * POST_SD^2)) / 
                              (PRE_n + POST_n - 2))) * 
                         (1 - (3 / (4 * (PRE_n + POST_n - 2) - 1))),
    
    # Hedge's g for MID to POST with bias correction
    hedges_g_mid_post = ((POST_Mean - MID_Mean) / 
                         sqrt((((MID_n - 1) * MID_SD^2) + ((POST_n - 1) * POST_SD^2)) / 
                              (MID_n + POST_n - 2))) * 
                         (1 - (3 / (4 * (MID_n + POST_n - 2) - 1)))
  ) %>%
  select(-PRE_Mean, -PRE_SD, -PRE_n, -MID_Mean, -MID_SD, -MID_n, -POST_Mean, -POST_SD, -POST_n)

# Create data in long format for analysis
mydata_reshaped <- mydata %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c("PRE", "MID", "POST"), names_to = "Timepoint", values_to = "Weight") %>%
  mutate(Timepoint = factor(Timepoint, levels = c("PRE", "MID", "POST")))

# Calculate p-values for VO2 measures
p_values <- run_paired_ttests_grouped(mydata_reshaped, "Measure")

# Join summary stats and p-values
summary_p_values <- left_join(mydata_summary, p_values, by = "Measure")

# Display significant results
p_values_filtered <- p_values %>%
  filter(if_any(starts_with("p_val"), ~ . < 0.05))
kable(p_values_filtered, digits = 3, caption = "Significant (p<0.05) Paired t-test p-values for PRE vs MID, PRE vs POST, and MID vs POST")

# Display full results with hedge's g
kable(summary_p_values, digits = 3, caption = "Summary of mean and stdev grouped by measure, Hedge's g and p-value")

# Export results
file_name <- paste0(output_directory, "/", date, "VO2_combined_summary.csv")
write.csv(summary_p_values, file = file_name, row.names = FALSE)

# TRAINED subjects analysis
mydata_summary_T <- mydata_T %>%
  group_by(Measure) %>%
  summarize(
    PRE_Mean = mean(`PRE`, na.rm = TRUE),
    PRE_SD = sd(`PRE`, na.rm = TRUE),
    PRE_n = sum(!is.na(PRE)),
    MID_Mean = mean(`MID`, na.rm = TRUE),
    MID_SD = sd(`MID`, na.rm = TRUE),
    MID_n = sum(!is.na(MID)),
    POST_Mean = mean(`POST`, na.rm = TRUE),
    POST_SD = sd(`POST`, na.rm = TRUE),
    POST_n = sum(!is.na(POST)),
    PRE_Mean_SD = sprintf("%.2f±%.2f", PRE_Mean, PRE_SD),
    MID_Mean_SD = sprintf("%.2f±%.2f", MID_Mean, MID_SD),
    POST_Mean_SD = sprintf("%.2f±%.2f", POST_Mean, POST_SD)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    # Hedge's g calculation with bias correction
    hedges_g_pre_mid = ((MID_Mean - PRE_Mean) / 
                        sqrt((((PRE_n - 1) * PRE_SD^2) + ((MID_n - 1) * MID_SD^2)) / 
                             (PRE_n + MID_n - 2))) * 
                        (1 - (3 / (4 * (PRE_n + MID_n - 2) - 1))),
    hedges_g_pre_post = ((POST_Mean - PRE_Mean) / 
                         sqrt((((PRE_n - 1) * PRE_SD^2) + ((POST_n - 1) * POST_SD^2)) / 
                              (PRE_n + POST_n - 2))) * 
                         (1 - (3 / (4 * (PRE_n + POST_n - 2) - 1))),
    hedges_g_mid_post = ((POST_Mean - MID_Mean) / 
                         sqrt((((MID_n - 1) * MID_SD^2) + ((POST_n - 1) * POST_SD^2)) / 
                              (MID_n + POST_n - 2))) * 
                         (1 - (3 / (4 * (MID_n + POST_n - 2) - 1)))
  ) %>%
  select(-PRE_Mean, -PRE_SD, -PRE_n, -MID_Mean, -MID_SD, -MID_n, -POST_Mean, -POST_SD, -POST_n)

# Reshape trained data for analysis
mydata_reshaped_T <- mydata_T %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c("PRE", "MID", "POST"), names_to = "Timepoint", values_to = "Weight")

# Calculate p-values for trained subjects
p_values_T <- run_paired_ttests_grouped(mydata_reshaped_T, "Measure")
p_values_filtered_T <- p_values_T %>%
  filter(if_any(starts_with("p_val"), ~ . < 0.05))

# Join and display results for trained subjects
summary_p_values_T <- left_join(mydata_summary_T, p_values_T, by = "Measure")
kable(p_values_filtered_T, digits = 3, caption = "TRAINED: Significant (p<0.05) Paired t-test p-values")
kable(summary_p_values_T, digits = 3, caption = "TRAINED: Summary with Hedge's g and p-values")

# Export trained results
file_name_T <- paste0(output_directory, "/", date, "VO2_TRAINED_summary.csv")
write.csv(summary_p_values_T, file = file_name_T, row.names = FALSE)

# UNTRAINED subjects analysis
mydata_summary_U <- mydata_U %>%
  group_by(Measure) %>%
  summarize(
    PRE_Mean = mean(`PRE`, na.rm = TRUE),
    PRE_SD = sd(`PRE`, na.rm = TRUE),
    PRE_n = sum(!is.na(PRE)),
    MID_Mean = mean(`MID`, na.rm = TRUE),
    MID_SD = sd(`MID`, na.rm = TRUE),
    MID_n = sum(!is.na(MID)),
    POST_Mean = mean(`POST`, na.rm = TRUE),
    POST_SD = sd(`POST`, na.rm = TRUE),
```
